samples.comment:
I recieved assurances that the product would be delivered at a date that came and went. Nothing was done until I really started pestering your company and had to do my own research to figure out why the VAI
guitar amp was not delivered on the date assured and was finally delivered but considerably well after the date declared.

Bonjour
Impossible de me connecter…l’apairage en Bluetooth se fait pourtant 
Et puis une version en français serait bien.
Devin ne parles t-il-pas français ?
L Noel

I asked about an item that you have on sale (and are still advertising as such), but that you are sold out of: the 20% off spark2 pearl. Amazon and your US website still has stock. Your AI response was basically that they were sorry for my bad luck, but there is no way to get the sale price honoured when the stock comes in. It feels dismissive and I don’t quite understand why you are willing to loose a sale because of your inability to be sufficiently stocked. As I have been weighing my very competitive options for new gear, a 20% sale tipped the scale in your favour…and then it didn’t. I don’t expect anything more than an AI generated response to this, but I am curious WHY you are not able to honour your sale price for a customer who expresses a desire to purchase within the sale window? 
     I’m very disappointed and considering other options (e.g. Yamaha THR)

Positive Grid has some serious problems.  When orders go wrong like mine, PG makes is really hard to navigate getting some sort of resolution.  I ordered a Positive Grid 2 late 2025.  The first order was lost in transit by UPS (never reaching my State).  Since PG considers any order "mailed" as fulfilled, there is no tracking to make sure the purchaser receives the unit.  After my order was ten days late, I tried to get PG help.  The PG AI bot essentially blamed me for the lost order asking me to verify my address (which was correct) and to make sure I had a secure place to receive the order (and maybe try a different address).  Eventually, I live person contacted me and saw that UPS lost the item in CA and reissued the order.  The order finally arrived today and it was only the Spark Carry Bag - no Positive Grid 2.  As a repeat customer, I expect better than this type of treatment.  The AI bot is very weak (and I work in the AI field).  I resent a request to support to inform me what the heck is going on.  The online status of my order is still the original noted as FULFILLED.  The UPS tracking number on the order still says it is stuck in Ontario CA.  So, I paid for the item and now spend too much time trying hunt down the status of my order.  This is how PG treats a former customer.

Everything took a 2 day response. I lost the original email with the RA number and had to email again. I got an automated response immediately but then 2 more days for the real reaponse with the RA number. I love the products but you need better AI or you need an actual human monitoring these emails more.

The AI support was horrible. Said the capability was no available in BIas FX2 until i sent a screenshot showing that it does work. Once I got a "real" person and not a clanker, it was awesome support. Is the issue fixed? no but that is a server side capability that I just need to wait on. (analyzing chords for songs)

The process of having to set up a new account just to update an email address is ridiculous and makes your members website archaic in its function. I’ve had to update my email address across a large number of sites and your process was the dumbest and most annoying of all. Then you need support to link old and new. Incredibly frustrating and so unnecessary.

It´s a bit disappointing that a user makes a suggestion for an improvement (personal opinion) on a product (Spark2) and gets answered by AI. Not that the AI answer was unpleasant, but it just makes user feedback pointless. As a user of your products, I would expect a human response, not a machine one. I hope that the product never malfunctions, as considering this example, I don´t have any hopes for a solution, talking to bots.

"While I understand the utility of AI auto-replies for basic inquiries, they are not helpful in this situation. I always perform my own troubleshooting including AI before contacting support because my time is valuable as is yours..

This issue requires actionable solutions, not automated placeholders. I need a concrete timeline: for example, a confirmation that you will have a solution by a reasonable date for a firmware release that addresses the chirping issue.

If you cannot provide a substantive human response, I will be returning this product and switching to the JBL alternative. Relying on automation to delay real support is not sustainable and will drive customers away."

David (AI)

Hello, I thank the support people at positive grid for being responsive in trying to solve my chord analysis issues and I am happy to say that issue has improved. I can now analyze chords which were previously generating an error message and failing with the anlysis.
However, I now have a new issue. I now am having problems trying to add songs to my "saved songs" list. I select the song and try to add it to my playlist but the song does not show up in the playlist.
I was looking for specific info and the AI bot gave mostly generic info I already knew. I would prefer to interact with a real person and not AI in the future.
to much AI

Purchased amp on Saturday 29 Jan specifically because of the 20% off offer. Realised straight away that I’d ordered the wrong one so contacted customer services on the same day to try to prevent shipping and order the correct one. Was informed (by AI) that the amp had shipped on Saturday and then again by  customer service on Monday 2 Feb. Was then informed this morning Tuesday 3 Feb by Parcelforce that they had only just received and shipped the amp today. This means I will have to return or refuse delivery at a cost to myself, which surely could have been prevented! I was also informed that I could no longer get the 20% discount offer as it had expired. Not a good experience for my first purchase from Positive Grid.
Difficult to find help page, contact email, etc.  Also, only got an AI bot response.
I disapprove of AI replacing humans.

1. When its an ai answer why give email a name?
2. Sign off 'Best'. Best what? Best I could do?
3. Says AI can make mistakes. Humans have managed without this tagline, huh?
4. Spark Mini still not connecting with DAW.
oh well..

I don’t want to hear from a fuckass ai when I reach out to customer support. ChatGPT is at my finger tips. If I’m reaching out to the support email I want human responses. I don’t get why it has to be so dystopian
It took several emails to discover I was not communicating with a real person. At no time, or no where on the website did I find a phone number so I could speak to a real person.
No help at all! And I just paid for the "X" download and it will NOT work on my brand new Windows 11 laptop. WTF!?

Hi, 
I did not receive any real support as my remark was more to give a little feedback on my new Spark 2 and ask if possible to add some different styles in the jam section or looper drums section (like reggae, hiphop, punk rock,...) and also effects l, especialy a spring reverb.
The only answer I received is a A.I. email saying it will be forwarded further.
I realy hope Positive Grid will add th9se styles amd effects to upgrade the user experience and bring more possibilities to the players using the devices.
Definitly enjoy the Spark 2 and the possibilities it offers.
Thank you

He was very attentive and helpful in solving our problem
Already tried your “solution”.  It doesn’t work. You should’ve just entered the registration number for me don’t you think?

I use an acoustic nylon strings guitar. May be the spark mini is dedicated for metalic strings.
I have understand that it is a modélisation amp...

Didn’t quite understand why if a product goes on sale within 30d of when the product was purchased, a price adjustment is not done. 

I appreciate the $20 coupon offered but it would have been nice if there were more options. In fact I would have loved the option to return it and then be able to take afvantage of the sale and get a larger amp.
Difficult to find help page, contact email, etc.  Also, only got an AI bot response.
I have yet to hear back with a solution to my my issue so I can't give an opinion at this time
I disapprove of AI replacing humans.

It took several emails to discover I was not communicating with a real person. At no time, or no where on the website did I find a phone number so I could speak to a real person.

My RMA is taking longer than expected and hasn’t been resolved yet
The issue is still not fixed..

If you are using AI for response to inquiries then why does it take so long to get a response?  I'm not very happy to learn the XSonic foot controller for my Spark 40 was not made by Sonic and wasn't part of your regular product lineup.  I bought this controller when they first came out and there was no mention from Positive Grid that the foot controller was not made by the same company.  So it turns out I can't register the product with you because it wasn't made by you? I have had a lot of trouble with that footswitch, it is hard to get paired with my Spark 40 and is not an intuitive pairing process at all.  When it first came out on the market I was happy to hear it was available, since the amp did not have a footswitch previously.  i bought one when they first came available and have never had an easy time using it, so I never even tried to register it until recently, because I now have some time on my hands to master its usage but I am still frustrated with the difficulty in getting it to work with the amp.
I did not receive any support.

Hi!
I would first like to thank Eleanor, my representative. She is fantastic and did an amazing job to help me! The solution I chose was to replace my Spark 40 with a new Spark 2. I love the products. There was an internal defect in my Spark 40 that caused it to cut out every few minutes. After researching Spark forums online, it seems this is a common issue in my model and it was recommended that Positive Grid would take of it. While you did provide me the option of a certified repair, the cost of repair and shipping charges I would incur totaled to $250 - $275. Eleanor offered me a second option of purchasing the Spark 2 with a discount to help compensate for my inconvenience. The discount was the standard upgrade discount available to all Spark 40 owners and an additional $20 coupon. While I am grateful for her offering me that discount (she said it was the most she was authorized to provide) I would have preferred a larger discount towards additional products as $20 seems a bit light. I appreciate your products and team, and am enjoying my new amp and learning the new tools it has. I hope my message can help you continue to provide great customer service.
Thanks,
John Curiel
Terrible communication throughout

the ai was very friendly and quick but it didn't tell me if my wish would be technically possible. it just said i understand your concern. would be much nicer to wait a little longer for a reply but get it from a real person
Your cancellation process is basically non-existent. I cancelled an order almost immediately after making it and it was ignored and shipped anyway

Dear Devin,

​Thank you for your response.

​I would like to provide some technical feedback that might be helpful for your team. Despite the official documentation stating that the USB interface is only for Windows or Mac, I have successfully recorded high-quality digital audio and 4K video directly onto my Samsung Galaxy S24 Plus using a USB-C fiber optic cable. The phone recognizes the Spark LIVE perfectly as a professional audio interface.

​Furthermore, I found a solution for the backing track limitation: by connecting my iPad directly into the Spark LIVE's "Line In" port, I was able to mix the backing track with my guitar signal and send the full mix through the fiber optic USB cable to my mobile device. This setup resulted in a perfect, high-definition digital recording of both instruments.

​I believe this is a great workaround for mobile users who want to take full advantage of the Spark LIVE’s digital output without needing a PC or Mac. I hope this information is useful for your technical support database.

​Best regards,

​Marcelo Ortega.

It’ taken me a couple of weeks to cool off from the frustrations of using the Spark-2.   Can you imagine?   I traded in my only other amp for yours, however I couldn’t get it to work and when I finally did, I didn’t want to look at this stupid thing.  It’s been sitting for almost three weeks before I would try again.  Doesn’t bode well for your product.   I will describe as follows.

What sent me over the edge to the dark side: 
I spent over two hours trying to get your APP properly connected.  For over two hours I went back-and-forth over the procedures; rebooted my iPhone; rebooted your APP; but nothing would work.  I KNEW IT WAS AN EASY PROBLEM, so finally I went to your online contact for help.   
THIS IS WHERE THE NIGHTMARE started.   Now I have a whole new set of problems:  what the firmware levels, what type and software level of three other items?   ARE YOU GUYS OUT OF YOUR MINDS?  Is this how you help, by bringing more challenges and problems?   Thanks for nothing.  And you help service will not accept my email unless I answer all of your STUPID questions.  WHAT A NIGHTMARE!!!.   Let’s not help the customer; let’s piss-off them off!!!!   

So, I put B.S. answer into your system so that I could get through, and then it took four days to get a response.  FOUR DAYS!!!!!

THE ROOT CAUSE: 
Note:
•	I had successfully downloaded your APP onto my iPhone 14 and registered with your company.
•	I had successfully Bluetooth connected my iPhone 14 to your Spark 2.
(I wish I had screen shots, however I could not get back in to see the screens, so I have to describe from memory)
When I first opened your APP, the first page asks which Spark amp is being used.  Spark 2 >> done.  No problem
Then the next page connects the Bluetooth.  Done successfully.
But then the next page read that I needed to “connect the audio”.
Please note: I have no idea why/who/what/where/when “connect audio” means.   And you gave no instructions.
Then the next page (from memory) didn’t say much.   it had the words “connect audio”, however there were no instructions on what to do.   I thought “maybe if I touch the words “connect audio” that that’s a link/button.  I tried once, twice, three times I touched on the words “connect audio”, but nothing.   Over the next couple of hours, as I was frustratingly going back-and-forth, I came upon this page several times; and I would think/wish that the words “connect audio” was some sort of link/button to take me to connect audio.  I tried several times touching on the words “connect audio”, but nothing.  I was blocked.

After four days of waiting, I realized that the Spark company had no real intentions of helping me, I took your amp back to the music store (Guitar Center) from which I purchased it.  The store associate helped me.   He handled my iPhone, however he held it so that I could see what he was doing in your APP.   When he got to that third page, where the words “connect audio” were, I was immediately thinking “well, there’s nothing on this page to do”.  Can you imagine my shock when he did select some secret/trick button that brought us to the next page in your APP, to allow us to connect the audio.   Are you guys friggin kidding me??????   I was on that page numerous times, searching and looking for something to press.  OH!!!!!   It’s a trick/hidden button?!?!?!   You friggin morons.

So, now it was hooked up.

After a two week cool-down, I finally decided that I would give it another try, so I turned on your amp and APP.  The first thing your APP asks for is for me to register the product.   OK.   And quickly it asked for me to enter the serial number.   AND THE NIGHTMARE CONTINUES.    I entered the serial number: Nope, that’s not right.   Tried again:  Nope, that’s not right.   Tried again: Nope, that’s not right.     I just want to cry.   What is it with you people and these confusing systems?  


  ((I had photos here, but they do not print))


Here is my serial number, part number tag, along with your further instructions.
  

Ya-know, if you made it easier, you wouldn’t need instructions.
These instructions are absolutely meaningless to me.
I have read them a dozen times, in amusement as to how someone tries to explain something, but doesn’t help at all.
Which bullet point applies to me?   IDK?!?!?
Your examples are more confusing.
How about this, why don’t you use the proper format on your serial number tag and then there won’t be any problems.
Whatever.   I’ll sleep better WITHOUT registering.

I’ve worked more than thirty years in electronic control systems, with the last twelve years with radio/navigation units as a Tier-1 to the automotive companies.   All of these products have displays.  

Now, I’m going to use an acronym that your company has obviously never heard of before:  
HMI (Human Machine Interface): this refers to the systems and interfaces that allow humans to interact with machines or processes; through screens, control panels, or dashboards.  

You guys really need to become aware of and remove all of these little tricks, confusions and hidden meanings; so that your product is usable.   I suggest you read the book: The Inmates Are Running the Asylum by Alan Cooper: how engineers and programmers end up designing products for themselves, not for real users; why this leads to confusing, frustrating software.   Also: there are companies out there that specialize in helping companies like you improve their HMI.  I suggest you call them.

After three weeks, I finally for the first time played with your amp.   It sounds good.  I guess it has all kinds of other features, like the looper; but I think that I just don’t have the constitution to go beyond simply turning-on your amp and using it.  The idea that I will again and again and again and again and again be faced with confusions is too much to bear.  
What a nightmare.


Regards,
Mike Tramutolo

I was inquiring online about the pedal’s actuation by describing the way the pedal actuates on lifting your foot instead of when you press your foot and everything online I found said it should actuate when pressing, so I went to Positive Grid support to inquire and was told, I think by your AI assistant, that it was indeed to actuate when pressed and not when you lift your foot.  I was then contacted by a representative who asked for a video and then said it was working as designed, so now I’m confused and can’t find any literature on proper actuation of the pedal. I would like some sort of proper documentation, if possible, describing how it works.  I’ve had the pedal for over a year and really never used it because of this issue, until I recently started to inquire about the issue.
I have not been given a return label for shipping my amp back for replacement or any follow up after providing the RMA information

I met Dave (ai) who listed some actions to test to get BIAS working. That didn't work (i already found all those actions to try on user forums). When I reported back that it wasn't working it and how to continue it was radio silence for several days until I found a solution myself. When I reported that and requested to close the support case I got an answer from Dave (ai) within an hour, and this feedback mail within a day. Soo, some human interaction as 2nd level support and a bit faster replies would have been appreciated. Anyhow, all is good and working now :)
AI answer that made me lose all my presets
I still can't use dropbox for my backups
The person who was helping me was alright, he gave me some instructions to try fix my amp, however it didn't work.
I don't get a solution / support till now. I think, your feedback mail is too fast... :)

The AI was extremely clever; it took me a while to realise Devin was not a person.  However, Devin asked me to photograph my power supply, which I did, an then having establish it was okay, many further exchanges ensued.  Had it been a human being asking the questions, that person would have know to tell me to photograph the connector into the transformer, which would have immediately shown the power supply was the problem.  The problem would have been solved weeks earlier. 

The person who finally got involved identified the problem very quickly.
Muy mal todo
